title: Patch Hermes custom fallback on nuc so Fireworks fallback honors explicit endpoint config
description:
## Summary
Hermes on `nuc` does not honor `fallback_model.base_url` / `api_key_env` for custom OpenAI-compatible fallbacks (for example Fireworks). When Codex fails, Hermes logs that it is switching to the custom fallback but still sends the request to the Codex endpoint (`https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex`) and gets a 403.

## Findings
- Reproduced on `nuc` with the Telegram-backed Hermes deployment.
- Upstream matches issue #3124 and related PRs #3182, #3853, and #4163.
- The pinned `openclaw-workspace` Hermes module uses `hermesAgent.packages.${pkgs.system}.default`, so pkgs overlays do not affect the deployed package.

## Local fix prepared
- Added a root `hermesAgent` flake input pinned to the same Hermes revision already used by `openclaw-workspace`.
- Made `openclaw-workspace.inputs.hermesAgent` follow that root input.
- Overrode `services.hermes-agent.package` on `hosts/nuc/default.nix` with a locally patched Hermes derivation.
- Added `patches/hermes-agent/0001-forward-fallback-explicit-endpoint-config.patch`, which forwards `fallback_model.base_url` and `fallback_model.api_key_env` into `resolve_provider_client()` and adds a regression assertion in `tests/test_fallback_model.py`.

## Remaining work
- Build/deploy the patched Nix configuration to `nuc`.
- Verify Fireworks fallback now hits `https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1` instead of the Codex endpoint.
- Decide whether to Nix-manage `FIREWORKS_API_KEY` instead of relying on the stateful Hermes `.env`.

## References
- Upstream issue: #3124
- Related PRs: #3182, #3853, #4163
- Dotfiles changes currently in working tree: `flake.nix`, `flake.lock`, `hosts/nuc/default.nix`, `patches/hermes-agent/0001-forward-fallback-explicit-endpoint-config.patch`

comments.author: emiller
comments.text:
Implementation start:
- Resuming from the local Nix patch already staged in dotfiles.
- Next steps are to finish any missing config wiring, deploy the NixOS config to `nuc`, and run real fallback tests on the deployed service/binary.
- I will append bead notes as deployment and verification progress.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text:
Pre-deploy notes:
- Verified the patched Hermes derivation is selected for `nixosConfigurations.nuc.config.services.hermes-agent.package`.
- The local flake now pins a root `hermesAgent` input to the same upstream revision as the existing openclaw-workspace lock and makes `openclaw-workspace.inputs.hermesAgent` follow it, so the local patch is applied without changing the Hermes base revision.
- Next: run a targeted dry activation for `nuc`, then deploy for real and test fallback behavior on the host.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text:
Dry-activate attempt failed during remote build on `nuc`.

Failure details:
- The Hermes patch was copied to the remote build as expected.
- The remote builder applied patches with strip settings that did not match the git-style patch headers (`a/run_agent.py`, `b/run_agent.py`).
- Result: `can't find file to patch` for both `run_agent.py` and `tests/test_fallback_model.py`.

Next action:
- Adjust the Nix override to set explicit patch strip flags (`-p1`) for the Hermes package, then retry dry activation and deployment.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text:
Follow-up on the failed retry:
- Adding `patchFlags = ["-p1"]` to the derivation override did not affect the builder behavior for this package.
- I am switching the override to apply the local Hermes patch explicitly in `postPatch` instead of relying on the package's implicit patch phase behavior.
- This keeps the patch file in-tree but makes application deterministic for the current upstream packaging.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text:
Implementation update:
- The upstream Hermes Nix package is only a thin wrapper around a prebuilt Python virtualenv (`hermes-agent-env`), so source-level `patches` on the wrapper derivation do not reach the actual `run_agent.py` implementation.
- I replaced the host override with a patched wrapper derivation that:
  1. copies the upstream Hermes wrapper package,
  2. copies the referenced virtualenv with symlinks dereferenced,
  3. rewrites the fallback snippet inside `run_agent.py`, and
  4. repoints the wrapper scripts to the patched virtualenv.
- This keeps the deployed fix minimal while avoiding a full rebuild of the Python dependency graph.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text:
Dry activation now succeeds for `nuc`.

Working package strategy:
- parse the upstream wrapper to locate the referenced `hermes-agent-env`,
- copy that virtualenv with symlinks dereferenced,
- rewrite `run_agent.py` inside the copied venv, and
- repoint the `hermes`, `hermes-agent`, and `hermes-acp` wrappers to the patched venv plus the copied package path.

Planned next step: perform the real deploy and then validate the fix on-host with a forced Fireworks fallback test.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text: Deployment succeeded via deploy-rs. Next I am validating on `nuc` that the live Hermes binary now uses the patched package and that a forced custom Fireworks fallback works in a real execution path.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text:
Post-deploy runtime test result:
- The deployed package is active and the patched `run_agent.py` is present in the copied venv.
- A forced fallback test on `nuc` still failed exactly in the original way: after the bogus Codex model exhausted retries, Hermes switched to `custom` Fireworks but still targeted `https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/` and got HTTP 403.
- Conclusion: forwarding explicit fallback endpoint/auth values from `run_agent.py` alone is insufficient on this pinned Hermes revision; another layer still collapses `custom` fallback traffic back onto Codex.
- I am now inspecting `agent/auxiliary_client.py` in the deployed build to patch the next missing resolution step and will re-test on-host.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text:
Root cause for the failed first post-deploy test:
- The outer wrapper pointed at the copied venv, but the copied venv's console scripts (`bin/hermes`, etc.) still had shebangs targeting the original upstream venv in `/nix/store/...-hermes-agent-env`.
- That meant Hermes was still importing the original unpatched Python modules at runtime.
- I am now rewriting copied venv bin-script shebangs to the copied venv path, then redeploying and re-running the forced fallback test.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text:
Simplifying the deployment approach again:
- Copying the full venv was unnecessary and brittle because the copied console scripts and venv metadata still resolved back into the original upstream store path.
- New approach: keep the upstream Hermes package intact, add a tiny Python overlay directory containing a patched `run_agent.py`, and inject that overlay via `PYTHONPATH` in the outer wrapper scripts.
- This should guarantee the runtime imports the patched module without fighting Python venv internals.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text:
Final implementation/testing notes:
- Deployment is now on `nuc` with `/run/current-system/sw/bin/hermes` resolving to the patched Nix package `/nix/store/1sqbyfp4hy5riip45vx8sfw3rni86b8f-hermes-agent-0.1.0-fallback-endpoint-patched/bin/hermes`.
- The final Nix strategy is a wrapper overlay, not a full source rebuild: it copies the upstream Hermes wrapper package, adds a patched `run_agent.py` under `lib/hermes-overlay/`, and injects that overlay via `PYTHONPATH` in the wrapper scripts.
- Verified on-host that importing `run_agent` with the deployed overlay resolves to the patched overlay file.
- Verified on-host with a direct `AIAgent` instantiation against the deployed code that fallback activation now swaps the client to `https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1/` with `api_mode=chat_completions` and `_client_kwargs.base_url` set to the Fireworks endpoint.
- Verified with a direct API call from the deployed code path that the fallback client actually sends requests to Fireworks: using a dummy Fireworks/OpenAI-compatible key returns a Fireworks 401 (`UNAUTHORIZED`) from `https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1/`, which is the expected proof that traffic no longer routes to Codex in that path.
- Remaining wrinkle: a full `hermes chat` CLI forced-fallback test in a temp home still printed the old Codex endpoint in its surfaced error output, so there may be an additional CLI/runtime path or logging discrepancy beyond the core `AIAgent` fallback path. Also, the live service environment currently does not contain `FIREWORKS_API_KEY`, so the Fireworks fallback is still not ready to enable in production config without supplying that secret.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text: New follow-up: wiring a real `FIREWORKS_API_KEY` into agenix/Nix for `nuc` so production Hermes can receive it as a managed secret instead of relying on ad-hoc state.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text:
Secret wiring implementation:
- Added `hosts/nuc/secrets/fireworks-api-key.age` to agenix, encrypted for both `edmundmiller` and `nuc`.
- Added the corresponding `fireworks-api-key.age` recipient rule to `hosts/nuc/secrets/secrets.nix`.
- Added `FIREWORKS_API_KEY` to the generated Hermes environment file on `nuc` by extending `hermesScintillateSecrets` in `hosts/nuc/default.nix`.
- Next step: deploy and verify `/run/agenix/fireworks-api-key` and `/run/hermes-scintillate-env/secrets.env` on `nuc`.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text: First secret deploy attempt rolled back cleanly because the new `fireworks-api-key.age` file was still untracked locally, so deploy-rs copied a store snapshot that referenced the secret in `secrets.nix` but did not include the encrypted file itself. I staged the new `.age` file and am retrying deployment.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text: Resuming after the interrupted deploy. Local disk space has been cleared; retrying deployment now that the new `fireworks-api-key.age` file is staged and available to the flake snapshot.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text: Discovered that `services.hermes-agent.environmentFiles` was not ending up in the rendered systemd unit for this upstream Hermes module, so merely generating `/run/hermes-scintillate-env/secrets.env` was not enough. I updated the activation script to also synchronize the managed secret set into `/var/lib/hermes-scintillate/.hermes/.env` with correct ownership, which is the file Hermes actually reads at runtime.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text: The first `.env` sync deploy failed and rolled back because the activation environment did not have bare `sed` on PATH. I replaced the line-removal logic with an explicit `${pkgs.python3}/bin/python` helper in the activation script to keep the merge deterministic and self-contained.

comments.author: emiller
comments.text:
Verification after successful deploy:
- `fireworks-api-key.age` now decrypts on `nuc` to `/run/agenix/fireworks-api-key`.
- The Hermes secret materialization file `/run/hermes-scintillate-env/secrets.env` now includes `FIREWORKS_API_KEY`.
- The activation script now also writes the managed Hermes secret set into `/var/lib/hermes-scintillate/.hermes/.env`, and that file now contains `FIREWORKS_API_KEY` alongside the other provider keys.
- I restarted `hermes-agent.service` successfully after deploy.
- Important remaining limitation: the full forced-fallback `hermes chat` test still surfaces the Codex endpoint during custom fallback, so key wiring is complete but enabling production Fireworks fallback is still blocked by the remaining Hermes fallback-path bug.

title: Fix missing 'long' npm dep in nix-openclaw gateway build
description: openclaw-gateway crashes on NUC: @whiskeysockets/baileys imports 'long' but pnpm strict resolution doesn't symlink it. Hotfix applied (ESM loader via NODE_OPTIONS + systemd drop-in) but wiped on next rebuild. Proper fix: add long symlink in nix-openclaw gateway-install.sh following existing pattern (strip-ansi, combined-stream, hasown).
notes:
HOTFIX APPLIED: ESM loader + systemd drop-in on NUC (~/.openclaw/fix-long-*.mjs, fix-long-env.sh, fix-long.conf). Works but wiped on rebuild.

BUMPING FLAKE WON'T FIX: nix-openclaw only has automated pin-bumps, no install script changes. gateway-install.sh still missing long symlink.

3 FIX OPTIONS:

1. PR to nix-openclaw: Add long symlink in gateway-install.sh (line ~116) following existing pattern for strip-ansi/combined-stream/hasown. ~30min. Proper fix but depends on upstream merge cadence.

2. Local Nix overlay: Override openclaw-gateway installPhase in dotfiles overlays/ to postFixup-add the long→baileys symlink. Self-contained, no upstream dep. Survives rebuilds. ~20min.

3. openclaw-workspace module patch: Add ExecStartPre to the NixOS module (module/default.nix in openclaw-workspace) that creates the symlink at runtime. Similar to existing findNodePath workaround. Survives rebuilds. ~15min but fragile if store path changes.

title: OpenClaw: configure skills-remote execution from NUC gateway to Mac
description: Gateway logs show 'skills-remote bin probe timed out (MacTraitor Pro @ 100.88.166.114)' on every startup. Ping from NUC to Mac works (0.9ms via Tailscale), so it's a port/service/firewall issue on the Mac side. The gateway needs the Mac to run a skills-remote agent/listener that the gateway can invoke for remote tool execution. Need to figure out: 1) What service/port does skills-remote expect on the Mac? 2) How to configure it in the openclaw nix module? 3) Is there a homebrew/nix package for the skills-remote agent? This would enable the NUC gateway to delegate tool calls to the Mac.
notes:
INVESTIGATION COMPLETE:

## Root Causes (3 issues found)

### 1. Mac gateway launchd service is broken (BLOCKER for node)
The nix-openclaw module creates a launchd agent running `openclaw gateway --port 18789`, but the Mac config has `gateway.mode=remote`. Result: gateway refuses to start, spams logs: 'Gateway start blocked: set gateway.mode=local'. The `openclaw node run` command tries to connect to localhost:18789 but nothing is listening.

**Fix needed**: Disable the gateway launchd on Mac (set `launchd.enable = false` in nix config) since we're in remote mode. The OpenClaw.app handles the remote connection itself.

### 2. No node service running on Mac  
`openclaw status` shows 'Node service: LaunchAgent not installed'. The node host service is what enables the NUC gateway to invoke system.run (skills-remote) on the Mac. The OpenClaw.app IS running and paired as a node, but the headless node HOST service (ai.openclaw.node launchd agent) is not installed.

`openclaw node install` fails with 'Nix mode detected; service install is disabled.'

**Fix needed**: Create a custom launchd agent for `openclaw node run` in the nix-darwin module. The nix-openclaw upstream module only manages gateway launchd agents, not node agents.

### 3. NUC CLI commands fail (secondary issue)
NUC CLI commands (`openclaw nodes`, etc) fail with 'Config invalid' because OPENCODE_API_KEY env var is missing. The gateway SERVICE works (env injected via ExecStartPre), but CLI invocations don't have the env. This is the 'Config invalid; doctor will run with best-effort config' error.

## Proposed Fix
1. Set `launchd.enable = false` for the Mac openclaw instance (no local gateway needed in remote mode)
2. Add a custom launchd agent for `openclaw node run` in the Mac openclaw module
3. The node host will connect to the local gateway — but since we don't have a local gateway, we may need to configure the node to use the remote gateway URL directly, or run a local gateway in a compatible mode

Create tmux-gitu package with smart VCS detection
description:
# tmux-gitu Package Design

## Overview
Create a tmux plugin package that provides popup-based git TUI with smart VCS detection.
- **gitu** for git repositories
- **jjui** for jj repositories (when available)

## Architecture

### File Structure
```
packages/tmux-gitu/
├── default.nix           # Nix package definition
├── tmux-gitu.tmux        # Main tmux plugin entry
└── scripts/
    ├── helpers.sh        # Tmux option helper
    ├── open-git-tui.sh   # Smart launcher (detects VCS type)
    ├── open-gitu.sh      # Direct gitu launcher
    └── open-jjui.sh      # Direct jjui launcher
```

### Smart VCS Detection Logic

`scripts/open-git-tui.sh`:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
CURRENT_PATH="$(tmux display-message -p "#{pane_current_path}")"

# Detect VCS type
if [ -d "$CURRENT_PATH/.jj" ]; then
  VCS_TUI="jjui"
elif [ -d "$CURRENT_PATH/.git" ] || git -C "$CURRENT_PATH" rev-parse --git-dir >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  VCS_TUI="gitu"
else
  # Fallback to gitu if no VCS detected
  VCS_TUI="gitu"
fi

# Launch in popup
tmux display-popup -d "$CURRENT_PATH" -xC -yC -w80% -h80% -E "$VCS_TUI"
```

### Keybindings
- `G` - Smart launcher (auto-detects jj vs git)
- Optional: Direct launchers if user wants explicit control

### Nix Package

```nix
{ lib, stdenvNoCC, makeWrapper, gitu, jjui ? null }:

stdenvNoCC.mkDerivation rec {
  pname = "tmux-gitu";
  version = "0.1.0";

  src = ./.;

  nativeBuildInputs = [ makeWrapper ];

  installPhase = ''
    mkdir -p $out/share/tmux-plugins/tmux-gitu
    cp -r * $out/share/tmux-plugins/tmux-gitu/
    
    # Wrap scripts with TUIs in PATH
    for script in $out/share/tmux-plugins/tmux-gitu/scripts/*.sh; do
      wrapProgram "$script" \
        --prefix PATH : ${lib.makeBinPath ([ gitu ] ++ lib.optional (jjui != null) jjui)}
    done
    
    chmod +x $out/share/tmux-plugins/tmux-gitu/tmux-gitu.tmux
  '';

  meta = with lib; {
    description = "Smart tmux popup for git TUIs (gitu/jjui)";
    platforms = platforms.unix;
  };
}
```

### Integration

In `config/tmux/config`:
```tmux
# Load plugin
run-shell ${pkgs.tmux-gitu}/share/tmux-plugins/tmux-gitu/tmux-gitu.tmux

# Customize keybinding (optional)
set -g @open-git-tui 'G'  # Smart launcher
```

## Implementation Notes

1. **VCS Detection Priority:**
   - Check `.jj` directory first (jj repos)
   - Fall back to `.git` or `git rev-parse` (git repos)
   - Default to gitu if no VCS detected

2. **Popup Behavior:**
   - Centered (-xC -yC)
   - 80% width/height
   - Uses current pane's directory
   - `-E` flag closes popup on command exit

3. **jjui Integration:**
   - Optional dependency (jjui ? null)
   - Gracefully degrades if jjui unavailable
   - Only included in PATH if provided

4. **Future Enhancements:**
   - Toggle behavior (close if already open)
   - Configurable popup size
   - Support for other TUIs (tig, lazygit, etc.)
   - Per-project TUI preferences

## Why Package vs Inline?

**Inline approach (current):**
```tmux
bind G display-popup -d "#{pane_current_path}" -xC -yC -w80% -h80% -E gitu
```

**Package approach (this design):**
- Reusable across configs
- Smart VCS detection
- Easier to extend with more TUIs
- Toggle behavior possible
- Consistent interface for multiple tools

## Decision

**Current:** Use inline approach for simplicity.
**Future:** Implement package when jjui support becomes important or when more features needed (toggle, multi-TUI support, etc.).

title: Fix Raycast app discovery for Nix-managed macOS apps
description: Raycast surfaces ugly /nix/store/... paths (seen with Ghostty/OpenClaw) instead of clean app names/locations. Investigate long-term fix across nix-darwin/home-manager app exposure: avoid symlink-target path leakage while keeping declarative app installs.
notes:
Root cause: Home Manager on Darwin was using targets.darwin.linkApps (because home.stateVersion=24.11), which creates ~/Applications/Home Manager Apps as a symlink into /nix/store/home-manager-files and each .app as symlink to /nix/store package paths. Raycast/Spotlight surfaced those store-backed paths.

Fix: centralized in modules/options.nix for all Darwin hosts: set home-manager.users.<user>.targets.darwin.copyApps.enable = true; linkApps.enable = false; copyApps.enableChecks = false. This makes HM copy real app bundles into ~/Applications/Home Manager Apps (no store symlink path leakage). Kept nix-darwin /Applications/Nix Apps flow for system packages.

Also removed ghostty@tip cask from hosts/mactraitorpro/homebrew.nix and hosts/seqeratop/homebrew.nix; rebuilt host with darwin-rebuild; uninstalled lingering cask locally.

Verification:
- /Applications/Nix Apps/Ghostty.app exists and is a real dir (not symlink); Info.plist version 1.3.0.
- ~/Applications/Home Manager Apps/OpenClaw.app exists and is a real dir (not symlink).
- mdfind bundle-id queries return clean paths (/Applications/Nix Apps/... and ~/Applications/Home Manager Apps/...), not /nix/store app paths.

Raycast refresh note: if stale entries persist, restart Raycast (killall Raycast) and reindex Spotlight for app dirs (mdimport /Applications /Users/emiller/Applications).

title: Obsidian Insider CLI warning: installer/app mismatch (resolved)
description:
## Summary
Obsidian CLI emitted: "Your Obsidian installer is out of date ... better CLI support" while app logs showed `Loading updated app package ... obsidian-1.12.6.asar`.

## Root cause
Installer/wrapper and app package were on different tracks:
- `/Applications/Obsidian.app` wrapper metadata was old (1.8.9), which triggers CLI compatibility warning logic.
- Insider auto-updater had already downloaded and loaded newer app package (`obsidian-1.12.6.asar`).

## Resolution
- Removed `"obsidian"` cask from `hosts/mactraitorpro/homebrew.nix` to avoid stable-track overwrite.
- Uninstalled Homebrew Obsidian cask.
- Rebuilt nix-darwin config (`./bin/hey re`) and verified Obsidian absent from managed casks.
- Reinstalled Obsidian via Early Access (Insider) installer manually.

## Verification
- `obsidian --help` no longer prints installer mismatch warning.
- CLI command list renders normally.
- App still loads updated Insider package (`obsidian-1.12.6.asar`) and reports up-to-date.

## Follow-up
Keep Obsidian manually managed for Insider use; avoid re-adding the stable Homebrew cask in host config.
notes:
## How to check next time
Run these commands:

```bash
# Wrapper/installer version
defaults read /Applications/Obsidian.app/Contents/Info CFBundleShortVersionString
defaults read /Applications/Obsidian.app/Contents/Info CFBundleVersion

# CLI path in use
which -a obsidian

# Look for mismatch warning
obsidian --help | head -n 20
obsidian commands | head -n 20

# Confirm runtime package being loaded
# (usually shows updated Insider asar if Early Access is active)
tail -n 30 ~/Library/Application\ Support/obsidian/obsidian.log
```

Interpretation:
- If CLI prints `Your Obsidian installer is out of date... better CLI support`, the wrapper/installer is stale.
- If logs show `Loading/Loaded updated app package ... obsidian-<version>.asar`, the JS app package may still be newer than the wrapper.
- If using Insider/Early Access, prefer manual installer management and avoid the stable Homebrew cask.
close_reason: Resolved: removed Homebrew-managed Obsidian, rebuilt nix config, manually installed Early Access wrapper, and verified warning is gone.

title: Investigate opensessions tmux resize-sidebars loop that locks tmux
description: User reports tmux lockups with repeated hook errors: 'curl -s -o /dev/null -X POST http://127.0.0.1:7391/resize-sidebars ... returned 1'. Need root cause and durable fix (no workaround-only answer).
notes:
Initial findings: opensessions logs show continuous POST /resize-sidebars events for panes with pane_width == window_width (e.g. %28|home|@1|157|157, %27|home|@0|157|157). This strongly suggests orphan/full-window panes titled 'opensessions' trigger resize enforcement loops.
Reproduced in isolation: created a one-pane tmux window titled 'opensessions' (pane_width==window_width), installed after-resize-pane hook, triggered one resize event -> 15 POST /resize-sidebars requests in ~0.6s. Log showed repeated ctx for same pane (%12 @10 width 224/224), proving self-sustaining resize loop in server resizeSidebars enforcement.
Implemented durable fix in packaging layer: added packages/opensessions/patches/0003-prevent-full-window-sidebar-resize-loop.patch and wired it in packages/opensessions/default.nix. Fix skips resize enforcement for full-window opensessions panes (pane.width >= pane.windowWidth), preventing after-resize-pane feedback loops. Validation: nix build .#opensessions succeeds with patch series (0001-0003). Runtime repro test after applying equivalent code change: triggering a full-window opensessions pane generated only 1 POST /resize-sidebars (expected trigger) instead of storm; server logged skip guard hits.

5. **Redeploy**: `hey nuc`

title: jut: add branch subcommand for bookmark/stack management
description:
Add `jut branch` for the core GitButler workflow: create parallel branches, create stacked branches, and list/manage branches.

## Commands

### `jut branch <name>` (create parallel branch)
Creates a new branch from trunk (or specified base), sets a bookmark, and moves working copy there.
Maps to: `jj new -r "trunk()" -m "" && jj bookmark set <name>`

### `jut branch --stack <name>` (create stacked branch)  
Creates a new branch on top of current working copy (dependent work).
Maps to: `jj new && jj bookmark set <name>`

### `jut branch --list` (list branches)
Show all bookmarks with their stack relationships and status.
Maps to: `jj bookmark list` + stack detection from status.rs

### `jut branch --delete <name>` (delete branch)
Delete a bookmark (and optionally abandon the revisions).
Maps to: `jj bookmark delete <name>`

### `jut branch --rename <old> <new>` (rename branch)
Maps to: `jj bookmark rename <old> <new>` (jj 0.37 uses set+delete)

## Design Notes

- Default `jut branch <name>` creates from trunk (parallel work pattern)
- `--stack` / `-s` creates from current `@` (dependent work pattern)
- `--from <rev>` overrides the base revision for either mode
- After creation, `jut status` shows the new stack
- JSON output: `{"created": true, "bookmark": "name", "change_id": "...", "base": "trunk|<rev>"}`
- Branch list should reuse stack detection from stack.rs for rich display

## Workflow Examples

```bash
# Start feature work (parallel to other branches)
jut branch feature/auth

# Create dependent work (stacked on current)
jut branch --stack feature/auth-tests

# List all branches with stack info
jut branch --list

# Clean up
jut branch --delete feature/auth
```

title: tmux-smart-name: capture pi-busy.txt fixture
description:
The status tests only have a pi-idle fixture. Need a real pane capture of pi while actively working (spinner visible, no idle footer) to add as tests/fixtures/pi-busy.txt.

Steps:
1. Open a pi session and trigger a long-running operation (e.g. 'read this large file and summarize')
2. While the spinner is showing: tmux capture-pane -p -S -200 > packages/tmux-smart-name/tests/fixtures/pi-busy.txt
3. Add to test.each in tests/status.test.ts: ['pi-busy.txt', ICON_BUSY]
4. Run bun test to confirm it passes

Without this fixture, a regression that made everything return ICON_IDLE would not be caught by fixture tests (only by inline unit tests against individual pattern strings).
notes: Lower priority now: tmux-status extension provides authoritative status via filesystem bridge, bypassing regex entirely for pi. Fixtures only matter for the regex fallback path (extension not installed). The fixture gap is no longer a regression risk for the primary code path.

title: bd init: leaner defaults — global gitignore + skip AGENTS.md when skill is present
description:
## Problem

`bd init` has two friction points, uncovered by running it in a fresh repo.

---

### 1. AGENTS.md: internal contradiction

`bd init` generates this AGENTS.md:

```markdown
# Agent Instructions
This project uses **bd** for issue tracking. Run `bd onboard` to get started.

## Landing the Plane (Session Completion)
[... 25 lines of mandatory workflow ...]
```

But `bd onboard` itself says:

> Keep AGENTS.md lean while bd prime provides up-to-date workflow details.

And outputs a 7-line stub:

```markdown
## Issue Tracking
This project uses bd. Run `bd prime` for workflow context...
```

And `bd prime` is already auto-injected at session start by the hooks that `bd init` installs.
So the generated AGENTS.md is:
- Contradicted by `bd onboard`'s own advice
- Redundant with `bd prime` (which the hooks inject automatically)
- Redundant with the beads skill (if globally installed)

**Fix**: `bd init` should write the `bd onboard` minimal stub instead of the fat AGENTS.md.

---

### 2. .gitignore: hooks solved this, gitignore didn't

The hooks in `.beads/hooks/` are thin shims:

```sh
exec bd hooks run pre-push "$@"
```

Hook logic lives in bd itself. Upgrade bd, behavior upgrades everywhere. No drift.

The `.beads/.gitignore` is committed verbatim. When bd adds new runtime files (e.g. `dolt-access.lock` added post-SQLite migration), existing repos silently miss the pattern until `bd doctor` catches it. `bd doctor --fix` can update the file, but only if you think to run it.

**Fix options** (increasing ambition):
1. The pre-commit hook already runs — it could silently update `.beads/.gitignore` to current version and stage it, mirroring the shim pattern
2. `bd upgrade` / `bd doctor --fix` auto-commits the updated `.beads/.gitignore`
3. Make `.beads/.gitignore` itself a shim: a tiny file that `include`s a bd-managed path outside the repo (global gitignore in `~/.config/bd/gitignore`)

---

## Current Behavior (observed)

- `bd init` creates: `.beads/` dir, `AGENTS.md` in project root, 5 hook shims, `.beads/.gitignore`
- Root `.gitignore` is NOT modified (good — this was already fixed)
- The hooks shim correctly — no drift risk
- The `.beads/.gitignore` is committed verbatim — drift risk on bd upgrades

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `bd init` generates the `bd onboard` minimal stub, not the fat landing-the-plane AGENTS.md
- [ ] `bd init` accepts `--no-agents-md` to skip AGENTS.md entirely (e.g. when beads skill is globally installed)
- [ ] Pre-commit hook (or `bd doctor --fix`) auto-updates `.beads/.gitignore` to current bd version patterns without prompting

title: tmux prefix+d picker opens blank
Bug: raw git commits fork jj history (beads, memory_commit, any git tool)
description:
ROOT CAUSE
In colocated jj+git repos, git HEAD always points to the PARENT of jj's working copy:

    ○  parent (P)     ← git HEAD
    @  working copy   ← jj @ (child of P)

Any tool that runs 'git commit' creates a NEW child of P — a sibling of @:

    ○  P
    ├─ @  jj working copy  (jj made this)
    └─ ○  bd sync commit   (git made this)

Instant fork. Every time. Structurally guaranteed — not a race condition.

AFFECTED TOOLS
- bd sync: commits .beads/issues.jsonl via raw git
- memory_commit: commits .pi/memory/ via raw git (if no nested .git)
- Any script/tool that runs 'git add && git commit' in a colocated jj repo

SYMPTOMS
- Unexpected branches in 'jj log' after bd sync or memory_commit
- Feature code gets stolen from jj working copy into a raw git commit
  (because 'git add .' stages everything, not just the tool's files)
- History requires manual cleanup (jj abandon) to linearize

CURRENT WORKAROUNDS
- .gitignore .pi/memory/ prevents memory_commit from touching main repo
- Run 'jj git import' after any raw git commit (band-aid)
- jj abandon orphaned commits after the fact

Reproduced in: /Users/emiller/src/personal/bugster
See design doc for fix proposals: .beads/docs/jj-git-colocated-fix.md
design:
# Design: Raw git commits in colocated jj repos

**Bead:** dotfiles-jmpq  
**Status:** proposal  
**Date:** 2026-02-21

## Problem

In a colocated jj+git repo (`.jj/` + `.git/`), git HEAD always points to the
**parent** of jj's working copy. Any tool that runs `git commit` creates a
sibling of the working copy — forking history every time.

```
Before:          After bd sync:
○ P  ← git HEAD  ○ P
│                 ├─ @ working copy (jj)
@ working copy   └─ ○ bd sync (git)
```

This is structurally guaranteed, not a timing issue.

## Affected tools

| Tool             | What it commits         | How often       |
|------------------|------------------------|-----------------|
| `bd sync`        | `.beads/issues.jsonl`  | Every sync      |
| `memory_commit`  | `.pi/memory/`          | Every save      |

## Fix proposals

### 1. jj-aware wrapper for bd sync (recommended, immediate)

Detect `.jj/` in the repo root. If present:

```bash
# Option A: commit through jj instead of git
jj describe -m "bd sync: $(date)"
jj new

# Option B: post-hoc import
git add .beads/ && git commit -m "bd sync"
jj git import
jj rebase -s <jj-working-copy> -d <new-git-commit>
```

Option A is cleaner — jj manages the commit. bd sync just writes the file,
then jj picks it up naturally.

Could be a jut plugin, a bd config option, or a shell wrapper:

```bash
# ~/.config/zsh/functions/bd
bd() {
  command bd "$@"
  if [[ "$1" == "sync" ]] && [[ -d .jj ]]; then
    jj git import 2>/dev/null
  fi
}
```

### 2. Move .beads/ out of project repo

Store beads DB in `~/.local/share/beads/<project>/` instead of
`<project>/.beads/`. Beads syncs via its own mechanism, not git.

Pro: zero interaction with project VCS.  
Con: beads tasks not co-located with code; harder to share via PR.

### 3. Global memory (for memory_commit)

Move pi memory from `<project>/.pi/memory/` to `~/.pi/memory/`.
Memory is user context, not project code — it belongs globally.

Stopgap: `.gitignore` the `.pi/memory/` directory (already done in bugster).

### 4. Upstream fixes

- **beads**: add `vcs_backend = "jj"` config option, use jj commands
- **pi**: global memory option, or detect jj and skip git commit
- **jj**: no fix needed — this is working as designed

## Recommendation

**Short-term:** Shell wrapper for `bd` that runs `jj git import` after sync (option 1B).  
**Medium-term:** File upstream issues on beads + pi.  
**Long-term:** Both tools should detect jj natively.
notes: Pi upstream bug — raw git commits in tools fork jj history

title: ADR: opnix vs agenix for NUC secret management
description:
Architecture decision record evaluating opnix (1Password service account-based secrets) vs agenix (SSH key-based encrypted secrets) for the NUC homeserver.

## Decision: Keep agenix, don't adopt opnix

### Context
Evaluated github.com/brizzbuzz/opnix as potential replacement for agenix on the NUC. The NUC currently has ~20 agenix-encrypted secrets covering API keys, tokens, env files for openclaw, homepage, lubelogger, speedtest-tracker, bugster, hass, gatus, restic.

### Comparison

| Dimension | agenix (current) | opnix |
|---|---|---|
| Auth | SSH host key (exists) | Service Account token (new dep, paid) |
| Storage | tmpfs (/run/agenix/) | persistent disk (/var/lib/opnix/secrets/) |
| Network at boot | None — secrets in nix store | Needs 1Password API |
| Source of truth | Git (.age files) | 1Password vault (external) |
| Adding secret | agenix -e foo.age + secrets.nix | 1P vault + nix config |
| Rotation | Re-encrypt, commit, deploy | Change in 1P, restart |

### Dealbreakers for opnix on NUC
1. **Network dep at boot** — NUC should have secrets immediately; agenix embeds encrypted secrets in nix store
2. **Worse security** — agenix decrypts to tmpfs (wiped on reboot); opnix writes persistent disk
3. **Bootstrap chicken-and-egg** — service account token is itself a secret to manage
4. **Two systems** — password, restic, pre-boot secrets would stay agenix anyway
5. **Rotation isn't painful** — agenix -e + hey nuc is ~30s

### Where opnix would fit
- Dev shells (opnix env for nix develop) — orthogonal to agenix
- Teams sharing 1Password as secret store
- Ephemeral/CI without stable SSH keys
- macOS desktop (obsidian-sync) — but can't do TOTP, so still not viable

### Also evaluated for obsidian-sync (macOS)
opnix can't replace op CLI there either: needs service account (not desktop biometric auth), can't generate TOTP codes (time-based, not file-writable).
close_reason: Decision recorded: keep agenix. opnix evaluated and rejected — network boot dep, worse security (persistent vs tmpfs), bootstrap chicken-and-egg, would run parallel to agenix not replace it. Only interesting opnix feature is opnix env for dev shells (orthogonal, could add later).

- Previously completed tmproj/tw/twd/naming/metadata work remains foundational infrastructure.

title: Borrow tmux status line ideas from adibhanna
description:
Gruvbox-ish tmux status config from @adibhanna (https://x.com/adibhanna/status/2026333008731512861). Interesting bits to steal for our Catppuccin/dotbar setup:

## Config snippet
```tmux
# Status line customisation
set-option -g status-left-length 200
set-option -g status-left " #{session_name}  "
set-option -g status-style "bg=#3D3836"
set -g status-right "#{cd #{pane_current_path}; git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD} "
set-option -g window-status-format "#{window_index}:#{window_name}#{?window_last_flag,◂,}#{?window_activity_flag,♦,}#{?window_bell_flag,!,}#{?window_zoomed_flag,Z,} "
set-option -g window-status-current-format "#{window_index}:#{window_name}✓#{?window_zoomed_flag,Z,} "
set-option -g window-status-style "fg=#7c6f64"
set-option -g window-status-current-style "fg=#fbf1c7,bold"
set-option -g window-status-last-style "fg=#a89984"
set-option -g window-status-activity-style "fg=#7c6f64"
```

## Ideas worth borrowing
- Git branch in status-right (simple `git rev-parse`)
- Flag symbols: ◂ (last), ♦ (activity), ! (bell), Z (zoomed), ✓ (current)
- Could adapt symbols into dotbar or tmux-smart-name

title: Re-enable skipped sesh.zunit tests in CI
description:
In fix/zunit-ci-compat (commits 9be4d0c7, d3629f4f), 10 tests in config/tmux/tests/sesh.zunit were skipped to unblock CI. They need to be made actually runnable in the Nix sandbox.

## Skipped tests and why

**Binary availability (3 tests)** — sesh, fzf, zoxide not in nativeBuildInputs:
- 'sesh binary is available'
- 'fzf binary is available'
- 'zoxide binary is available'
Fix: add pkgs.sesh (homebrew-only, may need custom package), pkgs.fzf, pkgs.zoxide to nativeBuildInputs in flake.nix zunit-tests derivation.

**sesh list / tmux session tests (2 tests)** — no tmux server in sandbox:
- 'sesh list returns output'
- 'sesh list -t returns only tmux sessions'
Fix: start a headless tmux server in @setup (tmux new-session -d -s test), export TMUX, add pkgs.tmux to nativeBuildInputs, kill server in @teardown.

**zoxide-list.sh quality tests (4 tests)** — zoxide db is empty in sandbox:
- 'zoxide-list.sh returns output'
- 'zoxide-list.sh returns at most 30 lines'
- 'zoxide-list.sh excludes temp and noise dirs'
- 'zoxide-list.sh entries are real directories'
Fix: in @setup, create temp dirs and seed a fake zoxide db with 'zoxide add <dir>' for a few real temp paths.

**tmux popup test (1 test)** — no tmux server:
- 'sesh picker popup appears and closes cleanly on escape'
Fix: covered by the tmux server setup above (or keep skipped — fzf-tmux popup in a headless server may be impractical).

## Reference files
- config/tmux/tests/sesh.zunit — test file with skip guards
- flake.nix — zunit-tests derivation (nativeBuildInputs, build script)

title: Research: sesh vs sessionx tmux session managers
description:
Comparison of sesh (Go CLI, already installed) vs tmux-sessionx (tmux plugin). Sesh is the better fit — proper CLI, worktree-aware (sesh root), config-file sessions, multiplexer-agnostic. SessionX adds inline rename and window-mode but is bash/tmux-only.

## Comparison

| | sesh | sessionx |
|---|---|---|
| Type | Standalone Go CLI | tmux plugin (bash/fzf) |
| Install | brew install sesh | TPM plugin |
| Picker | BYO (fzf, gum, television) | Built-in fzf-tmux popup |
| Zoxide | Native | Optional |
| Preview | sesh preview | Tree view, window content |
| Rename | No (use tmux native) | ctrl-r inline |
| Window mode | No (sessions only) | ctrl-w shows windows |
| Tree mode | No | ctrl-t session+window tree |
| Config | sesh.toml (startup cmds, windows) | tmux options only |
| Multiplexer | Agnostic (tmux, zellij planned) | tmux only |
| Extras | Raycast ext, sesh last, sesh root | tmuxinator/fzf-marks |

## Decision
Stick with sesh. Already on prefix+t. Real issue is using one tmux session for everything — need per-project sessions.

## Action Items
- [ ] Set up per-project tmux sessions (dotfiles, obsidian, etc.)
- [ ] Consider sesh.toml config for common sessions with startup commands
- [ ] Integrate pi-sessions dashboard with sesh workflow
close_reason: Documented comparison. Decision: stick with sesh, focus on using per-project tmux sessions instead of one big session.

title: openclaw/linear integration runbook: device pairing + API key auth
description:
## Summary
Debugging session 2026-02-26: openclaw gateway not processing Linear webhooks (showing 'thinking' but no action).

## Root Causes Found

### 1. Device pairing (openclaw 2026.2.20 breaking change)
openclaw 2026.2.20 introduced mandatory device pairing for all WS connections. The local CLI/plugin device had a pending approval blocking all agent sessions.

**Symptoms:** gateway log shows `Error: pairing required` / WS 1008 close
**Fix:** `openclaw devices list` → find pending entry → `openclaw devices approve <requestId>`
**Note:** scope upgrades also trigger this — approve them too (look for `repair` flag in pending list)

### 2. Linear OAuth token expired
The `lin_oauth_*` agenix secret had expired/been revoked.

**Symptoms:** Linear GraphQL returns 401 `Authentication required, not authenticated`
**Fix:** Rotate token in Linear Settings → API; re-encrypt with agenix; `hey nuc`

### 3. lin_api_* vs lin_oauth_* Bearer prefix (fixed in linear-agent-bridge 0.1.3)
linear-agent-bridge hardcoded `Bearer ${token}` but `lin_api_*` personal keys must omit the Bearer prefix. Only `lin_oauth_*` OAuth tokens use Bearer.

**Symptoms:** Linear returns 400 `Remove the Bearer prefix from the Authorization header`
**Fix:** Released fix in linear-agent-bridge@0.1.3 — auto-detect token type

## Diagnostic Commands
```bash
# Check gateway logs
ssh nuc 'tail -50 /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-gateway.log'

# List/approve device pairing requests
ssh nuc 'source <(sed s/^/export / /run/user/1000/openclaw/env) && export OPENCLAW_AUTH_TOKEN=$(cat /run/agenix/openclaw-gateway-token) && openclaw devices list'
ssh nuc '... && openclaw devices approve <requestId>'

# Test Linear API key directly
ssh nuc 'curl -s -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql -H Content-Type:application/json -H Authorization:$LINEAR_API_KEY -d {query:{viewer{id name}}}'
```
close_reason: Documented. Linear: https://linear.app/minionsofmordred/issue/OPS-36

title: Fix tmux session naming to be smarter and integrated
description: Define a single canonical tmux session identity for the current project or worktree. Current naming is inconsistent across config/tmux/aliases.zsh, config/tmux/omarchy.zsh, config/tmux/toggle-tml.sh, and bin/wms. Session names currently come from basename(PWD), branch names, or hardcoded names like Work. This task should extract a shared primitive such as bin/tmux-project-name, optionally paired with bin/tmux-project-root, that normalizes the working directory via bin/git-worktree-cwd and returns a tmux-safe deterministic session name for both humans and agents.
design: Implementation sketch: add a shared helper under bin/ for canonical project root and session name resolution; make names stable, shell-friendly, tmux-safe, and worktree-aware; update existing entrypoints and layout helpers to call the shared helper instead of rolling their own naming; document the naming contract in docs/ade/tmux-ade-spec.md as implementation reality, not just target state.
acceptance_criteria: A shared helper exists for canonical tmux project and worktree naming; tmux entrypoints and layout helpers stop hardcoding their own session names for the main ADE path; worktree roots resolve consistently via bin/git-worktree-cwd before naming; the naming rule is documented clearly enough that an agent can infer it from repo docs and code.
close_reason: Added bin/tmux-project-root and bin/tmux-project-name, updated tmux layout/worktree helpers to use the shared naming primitive, added zunit coverage, and documented the naming contract in docs/ade/tmux-ade-spec.md.

title: pi-ai: store redacted_thinking blocks during Anthropic streaming
description: anthropic.js streaming handler has cases for text, thinking, tool_use content blocks but NO case for redacted_thinking. When API sends redacted_thinking, it falls through silently — block never added to output.content, lost forever. On next turn, message sent back without those blocks, API rejects with 400: 'thinking or redacted_thinking blocks in the latest assistant message cannot be modified'. Fix: add handler for event.content_block.type === 'redacted_thinking' that stores block with its data field (opaque encrypted blob). File: ~/.bun/install/global/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-ai/dist/providers/anthropic.js lines ~174-275

title: Rewrite tmux-smooth-scroll in Rust
description:
Rewrite azorng/tmux-smooth-scroll as a native Rust binary in packages/tmux-smooth-scroll.

## Problem
Upstream plugin (Perl + bash) spawns a new tmux subprocess per scroll line via system(). For a half-page scroll (~25 lines), that's 25 fork+exec+IPC round-trips adding 50-125ms of jank.

## Solution
Single Rust binary that:
- Talks to tmux via the control protocol or direct socket IPC (no subprocess per line)
- Handles easing math natively (sine, quad, linear)
- Reads @smooth-scroll-* tmux options for config
- Single invocation per scroll event: tmux-smooth-scroll down halfpage

## Key bindings (wire in tmux module)
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi J   run-shell -b 'tmux-smooth-scroll down normal'
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-d run-shell -b 'tmux-smooth-scroll down halfpage'

## Nix package
packages/tmux-smooth-scroll/ with default.nix using rustPlatform.buildRustPackage. Wire into modules/shell/tmux/default.nix.

## Reference
- Upstream: https://github.com/azorng/tmux-smooth-scroll
- animator.pl has easing math to port
- tmux control mode: tmux -C or libtmux for direct IPC
close_reason: Closed

title: No node host service on Mac — skills-remote probe times out
description:
NUC gateway logs: 'remote bin probe timed out (MacTraitor Pro @ 100.88.166.114)'. The Mac is paired as a node (roles: [node, operator]) and the OpenClaw.app is running, but the headless node host service (ai.openclaw.node LaunchAgent) is not installed. This is what enables the NUC gateway to invoke system.run / skills-remote on the Mac.

'openclaw node install' fails with 'Nix mode detected; service install is disabled.'

Fix: Create a custom launchd agent for 'openclaw node run' in the nix-darwin module. The upstream nix-openclaw module (github:openclaw/nix-openclaw) only manages gateway launchd agents, not node agents. Need to add a launchd.agents entry in modules/desktop/apps/openclaw/default.nix that runs 'openclaw node run' with the correct environment variables (OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH, OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR, OPENCLAW_NIX_MODE=1).

Key question: openclaw node run tries to connect to localhost:18789 (the local gateway). In remote mode there is no local gateway. Need to determine if: (a) the node host reads gateway.remote.url when mode=remote, or (b) we need a local gateway running too, or (c) there's another config to point the node at the remote gateway directly.

Depends on fixing the broken gateway launchd first (or at least understanding the architecture).

Files: modules/desktop/apps/openclaw/default.nix
Docs: https://docs.openclaw.ai/nodes/index, https://docs.openclaw.ai/platforms/mac/xpc

title: OpenCode TUI crashes over SSH on NixOS NUC
description:
OpenCode TUI (libopentui.so) crashes with SIGABRT when running over SSH on headless NixOS server (NUC).

**Upstream Issue:** https://github.com/sst/opencode/issues/6119

**Symptoms:**
- Immediate crash on TUI initialization
- SIGABRT signal from bun/libopentui.so
- Works fine with `opencode serve` (headless mode)
- Affects OpenCode 1.0.224 + Bun 1.3.5

**Environment:**
- NixOS 26.05 (hardened kernel 6.12.56)
- SSH session (no local console)
- TERM and TERMINFO_DIRS set correctly

**Workarounds:**
1. Use `opencode serve` for headless API mode
2. Access NUC via VNC/console directly
3. Run OpenCode locally with sshfs mount

**Status:** Waiting for upstream fix in opentui library.

title: tmux-smart-name: pi status indicators inconsistent
description:
Status icons for pi windows were unreliable — hard to tell what needed attention vs was working vs had failed.

Root causes found:
1. Priority ordering wrong: UNKNOWN beat WAITING in prioritize(). If one pi window showed ◇ (unknown) and another showed ■ (waiting for approval), the ■ was hidden. WAITING means 'I need a prompt' — should always win over UNKNOWN.
2. AGENT_PATTERNS.pi was dead code: the pi branch in detectStatus() exits before the generic patterns lookup, so that entry (including PI_BUSY_WEAK) was never used — just misleading.

Fixed in commit f1bd6dfe:
- Priority: ERROR > WAITING > BUSY > UNKNOWN > IDLE
- Removed dead AGENT_PATTERNS.pi entry
- Added real captured pi pane as fixture (pi-idle.txt) with parameterized test.each tests to prove parsing works on actual output
close_reason: Fixed in f1bd6dfe. Priority reordered, dead code removed, fixture tests added.

title: Bug: 4:47 AM Good Morning fired mid-sleep (2026-02-24)
description:
## Incident

2026-02-24 4:47 AM: lights came on, blinds opened, whitenoise stopped mid-sleep.

## Timeline (from HA recorder)

1. **22:00** — Winding Down fired → `goodnight=on`, both awake=off
2. **22:48** — Edmund's phone showed activity (post-bedtime). `edmund_awake_detection` fired → `edmund_awake=on` (no time guard existed, 22:48 passes anyway)
3. **04:47** — Monica's 8Sleep bed presence sensor registered off (brief movement). `monica_awake_detection` fired → `monica_awake=on`. With both awake + goodnight=on, `good_morning_both_awake` triggered → Good Morning scene ran.

## Root Cause

`monica_awake_detection` (and `edmund_awake_detection`) had no time guard. A brief bed sensor blip at 4:47 AM was indistinguishable from a real wake signal.

## Fix

Added `condition: time after: "07:00:00"` to both `*_awake_detection` automations and confirmed `good_morning_both_awake` already had the same guard (defense-in-depth).

Commits: 5f540bb1 (fix + VM tests), b048bb65 (goodnight invariant documentation)

## Design Decisions

### Time guard placement
The per-detection guards (on `edmund_awake_detection` and `monica_awake_detection`) are the first line of defense. They prevent the awake booleans from ever being set before 7 AM via automated triggers.

The time guard on `good_morning_both_awake` is the choke point and primary guardian of a stronger invariant (see below). It's not redundant — it's defense-in-depth.

### Goodnight invariant
Once `goodnight=on`, no automated path should clear it before 7 AM. Good Morning is the ONLY automation that clears goodnight, so its time guard enforces this invariant at a single choke point. Manual scene toggles bypass this intentionally.

This invariant is now documented in the code comment on `good_morning_both_awake` and tested explicitly (subtests: 'GOODNIGHT INVARIANT: stays on at 3 AM', 'GOODNIGHT INVARIANT: stays on at 6:59 AM').

### 22:48 PM signals are correct behavior
Edmund waking at 22:48 setting `edmund_awake=on` is expected and fine — 22:48 is after 7 AM so the time guard passes. The incident was caused by Monica's 4:47 AM signal, not Edmund's evening signal. The regression test preserves `edmund_awake=on` as a precondition to validate exactly this scenario.

### Regression test structure
Full forensic timeline replay in `time-guards-test.nix` (subtest: 'REGRESSION: Full 4:47 AM incident timeline replay'):
- Set goodnight=on at 22:00
- Fire edmund_awake_detection at 22:48 → assert awake=on (correct behavior)
- Fire monica_awake_detection at 04:47 → assert awake=off (fix verified)
- Assert goodnight=on (Good Morning never fired)
close_reason: Fixed in 5f540bb1 (time guards + VM regression tests) and b048bb65 (goodnight invariant docs + 6:59 AM boundary test). Both commits on main.

title: Epic: Port opencode-dcp features to pi-dcp
description: Port valuable features from opencode-dcp (https://github.com/Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning) to our pi-dcp extension in packages/pi-dcp/.

title: Turn protection — skip recent N turns from auto-pruning
description: opencode-dcp has turnProtection config (enabled/turns) that prevents auto-pruning tool outputs from the last N agent turns. Prevents the #1 DCP footgun: pruning something the model is actively using. pi-dcp has recency rules but no hard turn guard.
close_reason: Closed

title: Prune origins — audit trail for why items were pruned
description: Track WHY each item was pruned: which strategy triggered it, which message was the origin. Maps tool ID → {source, originMessageId}. Useful for debugging false positives and potential undo.

title: Model-specific context limits with percentage support
description: opencode-dcp supports modelLimits config with per-model context limits and percentage values (e.g. '80%'). pi-dcp uses a single contextLimit. Allows tuning behavior per model.

title: Sweep command — bulk prune since last user message
description: opencode-dcp has /dcp sweep that prunes all tools since last user message (or last N). Quick way to clean up after a messy exploration. Could be a /dcp slash command in pi.

title: Context analysis command — token breakdown visualization
description: opencode-dcp has /dcp context showing token breakdown with bar chart (system/user/assistant/tools). Helps users understand where context budget is going.

title: Prune notification modes (off/minimal/detailed)
description: opencode-dcp has pruneNotification config: off (silent), minimal (count only), detailed (full list). Also pruneNotificationType: chat vs toast. pi-dcp always shows details.

title: Improve system prompt — port opencode-dcp prompt patterns
description: opencode-dcp's system prompt is significantly better: timing guidance (manage at START of new turn not END), parallelization (don't call ONLY context tools), per-tool philosophy (distill=preserve, compress=phase boundaries, prune=last resort), policy_level=critical framing. See lib/prompts/system.md in upstream.
close_reason: Closed

title: Protected file patterns — glob-based protection for important files
description: Glob patterns for files that should never be pruned (e.g. **/.env, **/AGENTS.md, **/CLAUDE.md). pi-dcp currently has no protection — config/context file reads could get pruned.

title: Signature-based deduplication instead of content hash
description: opencode-dcp normalizes tool params (tool name + sorted params, ignoring output) to detect duplicates. pi-dcp uses raw content hashing. Signature approach is better: two read_file(foo.ts) calls with different results still dedup (older read is stale), more efficient than hashing huge tool outputs.

title: Write→Read supersede tracking
description: opencode-dcp prunes write outputs when a subsequent read captured the same file. pi-dcp only tracks write→write superseding. Read-after-write is the most common pattern — write a file, read to verify, write output now redundant.

title: Two-tier nudge: separate compress nudge for critical context limits
description: opencode-dcp has regular nudge ('clean up') and compress-nudge ('CRITICAL CONTEXT LIMIT, compress NOW'). pi-dcp has a single nudge. Two tiers give better urgency escalation.

title: Manual mode — user-controlled pruning toggle
description: User can toggle /dcp manual to disable automatic pruning. Tools only fire on explicit triggers (<X triggered manually> markers). Useful for users who want full control over context management.

title: Permission modes per tool (allow/ask/deny)
description: Each of prune/distill/compress can be independently configured as allow/ask/deny. Users who trust distill but not compress can set that. Currently pi-dcp has no per-tool permission granularity.

title: Session state persistence — survive restarts
description: opencode-dcp saves prune state to disk (~/.local/share/opencode/storage/plugin/dcp/{sessionId}.json) and reloads on restart. pi-dcp loses all state on restart. Saves: prune maps, compress summaries, token stats, tool params.

title: jut: enhance pull to update base (fetch + rebase + clean merged)
description:
Enhance `jut pull` to handle the full "update your base" workflow from the GitButler tutorial.

## Current Behavior
`jut pull` only runs `jj git fetch`. Missing: rebase onto updated trunk, clean up merged bookmarks.

## Desired Behavior

### `jut pull` (enhanced)
1. `jj git fetch` — fetch upstream changes
2. `jj rebase -d "trunk()"` — rebase all mutable revisions onto updated trunk  
3. Detect merged bookmarks — bookmarks whose commits are now ancestors of trunk
4. Report merged bookmarks to user (suggest cleanup)
5. With `--clean`: auto-delete merged bookmarks (`jj bookmark delete`)

### Flags
- `--clean` / `-c`: Auto-delete bookmarks that have been merged into trunk
- `--dry-run`: Show what would happen without doing it
- `--no-rebase`: Only fetch, skip rebase (current behavior)

### JSON Output
```json
{
  "fetched": true,
  "rebased": true,
  "merged_bookmarks": ["feature/done-work"],
  "cleaned_bookmarks": [],
  "conflicts": []
}
```

### Conflict Handling
If rebase creates conflicts:
- Report conflicted revisions in output
- Do NOT auto-clean those bookmarks
- Human/agent decides resolution

## Workflow
```bash
# Teammate merged your PR
jut pull              # fetch + rebase
jut pull --clean      # also remove merged bookmarks
jut status            # see updated workspace
```

## Design Notes
- Merged bookmark detection: `jj log -r "bookmarks() & ::trunk()"` finds bookmarks reachable from trunk
- Rebase strategy: `jj rebase -b "all:roots(trunk()..mutable())" -d trunk()` rebases all stacks
- Must handle case where trunk has not changed (no-op rebase)
- Must handle case where fetch finds nothing new
close_reason: Enhanced pull with fetch+rebase+merged-bookmark-detection, --clean/--no-rebase/--dry-run flags

title: jut: add oplog subcommand for operations history
description:
Add `jut oplog` for browsing jj operations history — the "go back in time" capability from the GitButler tutorial.

## Commands

### `jut oplog` (default: list recent operations)
Show recent operations with short IDs, timestamps, and descriptions.
Maps to: `jj operation log`

### `jut oplog --limit N`
Show last N operations (default: 10).

### `jut oplog --json`
Structured output for agent consumption:
```json
{
  "operations": [
    {
      "id": "abc123",
      "short_id": "abc1",
      "description": "commit 2 files",
      "timestamp": "2026-02-10T10:30:00-06:00",
      "snapshot": false
    }
  ]
}
```

### `jut oplog restore <op-id>`
Restore workspace to a previous operation state.
Maps to: `jj operation restore <op-id>`

## Design Notes
- Parse `jj operation log` with custom template (same RS/US separator pattern as stack.rs)
- Filter out snapshot operations by default (--all to include)
- Short operation IDs using same collision-extending scheme as revision IDs
- `restore` is destructive — require confirmation in human mode, just do it in --json mode
- Complements `jut undo` (which only undoes last op) with full history browsing

## Workflow
```bash
# See what happened
jut oplog

# Oops, go back to before that rebase
jut oplog restore abc1

# Agent recovery: find last good state
jut oplog --json | jq ".operations[] | select(.description | contains(\"commit\"))"
```

## Priority
P2 — useful but `jut undo` covers the most common case. This adds browsing/selective restore.
close_reason: Implemented oplog subcommand with list/limit/all/restore, short collision-extending IDs, snapshot filtering

title: Migrate CodeCompanion.nvim to v18 syntax

title: HA: remove deprecated temperature_unit/unit_system/name nulls from homeassistant config
description: NixOS HA module injects null defaults for temperature_unit, unit_system, and name into services.home-assistant.config.homeassistant. HA 2026.1.3 rejects temperature_unit as an invalid option, and unit_system/name with null values fail validation. These are set-via-UI-only in modern HA. Fix: override these to remove them from the generated YAML, or patch the NixOS module defaults. Found by build-time check_config validation.

title: Epic: HA automation testing infrastructure
description:
Build a test harness for Home Assistant automations so agents can verify automation logic without touching production. Motivated by the 4:47 AM Good Morning incident (2026-02-24) where missing time guards caused blinds to open and whitenoise to kill mid-sleep.

Goals:
- NixOS integration test that spins up HA with our config
- Mock clock to test time-gated automations (before/after 7 AM)
- State machine tests: set preconditions, fire triggers, assert outcomes
- Not required on every deploy, but runnable on-demand by agents
- Robust HA devex: agents can test changes before deploying to prod
close_reason: Closed

title: NixOS integration test: boot HA with our config
description:
Create a NixOS VM test (nixosTest) that boots a VM with our HA modules, verifies HA starts, and the generated configuration.yaml contains expected automations/scenes/input_booleans.

Acceptance criteria:
- nixosTest that imports our hass modules
- HA starts successfully in the VM
- configuration.yaml contains all expected automation IDs (edmund_awake_detection, monica_awake_detection, good_morning_both_awake, etc.)
- Run via: nix build .#checks.x86_64-linux.hass-config (or similar)

Notes:
- NixOS tests run in QEMU VMs, need x86_64-linux (can cross-build from Darwin or run on NUC)
- HA takes ~30s to start, test should wait for API readiness
- See nixpkgs/nixos/tests/ for examples
close_reason: Closed

title: Time-guard assertion tests for wake/sleep automations
description:
Once HA boots in the NixOS test VM, verify that time-gated automations actually block when they should.

Approach:
- Use HA REST API from the test driver (Python)
- Set system clock via timedatectl or faketime to simulate 3 AM vs 8 AM
- Set preconditions (goodnight=on, both awake=off)
- Trigger automation via API
- Assert: at 3 AM, awake booleans stay off (time guard blocks)
- Assert: at 8 AM, awake booleans turn on (time guard passes)
- Assert: Good Morning scene fires only when both awake AND after 7 AM

Test cases:
1. Wake signal at 3 AM → edmund_awake stays off
2. Wake signal at 6:59 AM → edmund_awake stays off  
3. Wake signal at 7:01 AM → edmund_awake turns on
4. Both awake at 3 AM (set manually) → Good Morning does NOT fire
5. Both awake at 8 AM → Good Morning fires, goodnight=off, scene activates
6. Edmund awake at 10:48 PM (post-bedtime) → stays off (the actual bug scenario)
close_reason: Closed

title: Nix eval test: assert automation conditions in generated YAML
description:
Lightweight nix-level test (no VM needed) that evaluates the HA module config and asserts structural properties of the generated automation list.

This is the fast, cheap test that CAN run on every deploy:
- Evaluate the NixOS config to extract services.home-assistant.config
- Assert automation IDs exist
- Assert time conditions are present on wake detection automations
- Assert Good Morning has both-awake condition + time guard
- Assert Winding Down scene resets awake booleans

Implementation:
- nix eval or nix build with a derivation that runs assertions
- Pure nix, no VM, runs in seconds
- Could be a flake check: nix flake check runs it automatically

This catches regressions like 'someone removed the time guard' without needing a full VM boot.
close_reason: Closed

title: HA test helper: API client + state machine assertions
description:
Reusable Python test helpers for HA automation testing in NixOS VM tests.

Provides:
- ha_api.wait_ready() — poll until HA API responds
- ha_api.set_state(entity, state) — set input_boolean/switch/etc
- ha_api.get_state(entity) → state string
- ha_api.trigger_automation(automation_id) — fire automation via API
- ha_api.set_clock(time_str) — change VM system time
- ha_api.assert_state(entity, expected) — assert with retry/timeout
- ha_api.run_scenario(preconditions, trigger, assertions) — full scenario runner

This becomes the foundation for agents to write new HA tests easily.
Package as a Python module available in the NixOS test driver.
close_reason: Closed

title: Regression test: 4:47 AM Good Morning incident (2026-02-24)
description:
Reproduce the exact bug from 2026-02-24 as a regression test.

The scenario (from postgres recorder forensics):
1. 22:00 — Winding Down fires: goodnight=on, both awake=off
2. 22:48 — Edmund wake signal fires (phone activity post-bedtime)
3. 04:47 — Monica wake signal fires
4. 04:47 — Both awake → Good Morning → blinds open, whitenoise off

Root cause: no time guard on wake detection or Good Morning automation after removing input_select.house_mode.

Test must verify:
- Step 2 is blocked (22:48 is before 7 AM guard)
- Step 3 is blocked (04:47 is before 7 AM guard)
- Even if both awake booleans are manually forced on at 04:47, Good Morning does NOT fire
- The same sequence at 08:00 DOES fire correctly

This is THE regression test for this bug. If it passes, the 3 AM incident can't recur.
close_reason: Closed
